title: The graph below shows the number of tourists visiting a particular Caribbean island between 2010 and 2017. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
prompt: The graph below shows the number of tourists visiting a particular Caribbean island between 2010 and 2017. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

paragraphs.text: The graph below shows the number of tourists visiting a particular Caribbean island between 2010 and 2017. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
paragraphs.italic_text: The graph below shows the number of tourists visiting a particular Caribbean island between 2010 and 2017. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.

paragraphs.text: Write at least 150 words.
The chart illustrates the number of visitors of a Caribbean island from 2010 to 2017.
The chart illustrates the number of visitors to a Caribbean island from 2010 to 2017, measured in millions.
paragraphs.italic_text: The chart illustrates the number of visitors to a Caribbean island from 2010 to 2017, measured in millions.

paragraphs.text: Overall, the number of visitors visiting the island increased steadily over the period.
Overall, the total number of visitors increased significantly over the period.
paragraphs.italic_text: Overall, the total number of visitors increased significantly over the period.

paragraphs.text: In addition, tourists staying on cruise ships recorded the lowest figure, at approximately a quarter of a million, while the number of visitors staying on the island remained below 1.5 million.
In addition, visitors staying on cruise ships started at the lowest level but grew rapidly, while those staying on the island increased more gradually and then stabilized.
paragraphs.italic_text: In addition, visitors staying on cruise ships started at the lowest level but grew rapidly, while those staying on the island increased more gradually and then stabilized.

paragraphs.text: In terms of visitors staying on cruise ships, it had a slight decline in 2012 which brought the number back to an all times low at around 0.25 million.
In terms of visitors staying on cruise ships, the figure declined slightly in 2012, falling to an all-time low of around 0.25 million.
paragraphs.italic_text: In terms of visitors staying on cruise ships, the figure declined slightly in 2012, falling to an all-time low of around 0.25 million.

paragraphs.text: However, the figure increased rapidly with a steady pace and reached the highest point, at 2 million, after surpassed the number of tourists staying on the island in 2016.
However, the figure then increased rapidly and reached a peak of 2 million in 2017, after surpassing the number of visitors staying on the island in 2016.
paragraphs.italic_text: However, the figure then increased rapidly and reached a peak of 2 million in 2017, after surpassing the number of visitors staying on the island in 2016.

paragraphs.text: Regarding visitors staying on the island, after stayed constant between 2010 and 2011, the figure started to grow gradually till 2013.
Regarding visitors staying on the island, after remaining stable between 2010 and 2011, the figure grew gradually until 2013.
paragraphs.italic_text: Regarding visitors staying on the island, after remaining stable between 2010 and 2011, the figure grew gradually until 2013.

paragraphs.text: In comparison with a significant climb of the number of visitors on cruise ships, the growing trend stopped and the figure became stable, only showed little drop in 2016 but recovered quickly. (169 words)
In contrast to the sharp increase in cruise ship visitors, this figure stabilized after 2013. It showed a slight decline in 2016 before recovering in the following year.
paragraphs.italic_text: In contrast to the sharp increase in cruise ship visitors, this figure stabilized after 2013. It showed a slight decline in 2016 before recovering in the following year.

paragraphs.text: 1. Grammar accuracy
You have several small errors:
“after stayed”
“after surpassed”
article/preposition issues
2. Lexical resource
Good range, but:
Avoid repetition (“visitors visiting”)
Use more natural collocations:
grew gradually
reached a peak
remained stable
3. Cohesion & sentence control
Some sentences are too long and unclear
Break them into shorter, clearer ones
4. Overview quality
Focus on main trends, not numbers
Include comparisons

The line graph illustrates the number of tourists visiting a Caribbean island between 2010 and 2017, measured in millions, and distinguishes between visitors staying on cruise ships and those staying on the island.

Overall, the total number of tourists increased substantially over the period. While visitors staying on the island initially made up the majority, the number of cruise ship tourists rose rapidly and eventually overtook them. In contrast, the figure for those staying on the island grew more moderately before leveling off.

In 2010, approximately 0.75 million tourists stayed on the island, compared to only 0.25 million cruise ship visitors, giving a total of around 1 million. The number of visitors staying on the island increased steadily to about 1.5 million by 2013, after which it remained relatively stable, with a slight dip in 2016 before recovering in the final year.

By comparison, the number of tourists staying on cruise ships remained low and even declined slightly in 2012, falling to around 0.25 million. However, from 2013 onwards, this figure rose sharply, reaching 1 million in 2014 and continuing to climb to 2 million by 2017. Notably, cruise ship visitors surpassed those staying on the island in 2016. (184)

model_answer_paragraphs: The line graph illustrates the number of tourists visiting a Caribbean island between 2010 and 2017, measured in millions, and distinguishes between visitors staying on cruise ships and those staying on the island.

Overall, the total number of tourists increased substantially over the period. While visitors staying on the island initially made up the majority, the number of cruise ship tourists rose rapidly and eventually overtook them. In contrast, the figure for those staying on the island grew more moderately before leveling off.

In 2010, approximately 0.75 million tourists stayed on the island, compared to only 0.25 million cruise ship visitors, giving a total of around 1 million. The number of visitors staying on the island increased steadily to about 1.5 million by 2013, after which it remained relatively stable, with a slight dip in 2016 before recovering in the final year.

By comparison, the number of tourists staying on cruise ships remained low and even declined slightly in 2012, falling to around 0.25 million. However, from 2013 onwards, this figure rose sharply, reaching 1 million in 2014 and continuing to climb to 2 million by 2017. Notably, cruise ship visitors surpassed those staying on the island in 2016. (184)
